Creator channel work

YouTube Video Editor Jobs

YouTube editor roles are not just cuts and exports. The strongest editors understand pacing, story shape, retention, sponsor reads, packaging, and the rhythm of a channel.

This category separates creator-channel work from general video editing jobs.

Join editor alerts Post a role early

Role examples

Early categories before live listings

Remote

Short-form video editor

Reels, TikTok, YouTube Shorts, creator teams

  • fast turnaround
  • caption styling
  • social hooks
Remote or hybrid

YouTube channel editor

Long-form YouTube, podcasts, education, creator-led brands

  • story pacing
  • retention edits
  • thumbnail collaboration
Contract

Brand video editor

B2B, ecommerce, product launches, paid social

  • motion basics
  • ad variants
  • stakeholder review
Entry level

Assistant editor

Post houses, agencies, production teams

  • media management
  • proxy workflows
  • Premiere or Resolve

What YouTube teams care about

Hiring teams often look for retention-aware editing, B-roll selection, thumbnail collaboration, title awareness, multicam workflow, sponsor integration, and a repeatable weekly cadence.

Role variations

Common roles include long-form channel editor, shorts editor, podcast-to-YouTube editor, faceless-channel editor, educational content editor, and assistant editor for a creator team.

Hiring teams

Post a video editor role early

Good listings should include content format, edit volume, software stack, compensation, timezone or location, revision cadence, and portfolio examples.

Connect this form to TalentPrism when the backend posting flow is ready.

Answers

Questions editors and hiring teams ask first

Do YouTube editor jobs require analytics experience?

Not always, but it helps. Editors who can discuss retention dips, pacing choices, hooks, and audience expectations tend to stand out.

Are YouTube video editor jobs remote?

Many are remote, especially for creator teams, but some channels prefer local or hybrid editors when shoots, media management, or producer collaboration happen in person.
